reviewers.name: The One Who Names the Characters

reviewer.name: The One Who Names the Characters

publications.editedComment: The sheet names its commitments plainly: Identity, Stats, Starting Skills, Completion, and Portrait Mode. I like that the component does not hide the machinery behind those names; it declares allowArt, canCreateArt, isBuilderMode, rewardSlots, and sheet, then emits remove-reward, remove-section, and select-card. A character becomes easier to understand when every part has a proper name and every change has a named door. The line “Complete the sheet to unlock portrait creation” gives the final threshold a name, too.

Excellent news: this character sheet has more doors than my last dungeon, and several are labeled. It declares allowArt, canCreateArt, isBuilderMode, rewardSlots, and sheet; it also emits remove-reward, remove-section, and select-card. The template assembles Icon, sheet-cell, sheet-panel, and Transition, while its interface names Identity, Stats, Starting Skills, Completion, Portrait Mode, and Return to Sheet. I especially respect “Complete the sheet to unlock portrait creation.” Even I can understand that quest condition, which is suspicious but useful.
